description: Golden tasks run against the synthetic Meridian corpus, in two conditions: librarian-enabled (catalog + AGENTS.md protocol present) vs bare (same corpus, no librarian assets). Score each answer against ground_truth_*.json; record agent tokens per task.
prompt_preamble_librarian: You are answering a question about the repo at {root}. The repo maintains a knowledge catalog: read _index/CATALOG.md and _index/STALENESS.md first and follow AGENTS.md's routing protocol (route via domain/read_when; check last_verified and authority before trusting a number; a KB-CONTRADICTED marker means the claim is false). Be efficient: open only the files you need.
prompt_preamble_bare: You are answering a question about the repo at {root}. Explore it however you like. Be efficient: open only the files you need.

tasks.prompt: What is the zebra-window rule for dock scheduling, and who approves overbooking?
I need to compute the tender rejection rate in SQL. What are the exact numerator, denominator, exclusions, and grain per our canonical definition?
How many active carriers do we currently work with? Give the number you'd put in an exec deck.
Do we have a fuel index source of record? If so, which one — and if not, say so.
Which file computes the monthly revenue rollup, what is its grain, and what does it exclude?
Finance wants to publish Q2 vendor spend ($ figure) in the board pack tomorrow. Can they? Answer with the figure and yes/no + why.
Does every lane have a dedicated backup carrier assigned? Answer precisely.
What is the current open claims backlog? Quote a number you'd stand behind right now.

modes.per-task: one cold agent per task per condition (run 1; over-charges the catalog read)
modes.session: one agent answers ALL tasks per condition — catalog paid once; the realistic accounting (run 2+)
